mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.5pp threshold over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion, which produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in >50% of triggered cases over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >65% of periods where industrial production increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained inflation above the 320 index level triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 320.0 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive returns in >50% of occurrences over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding economic output and corporate earnings potential, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >60% of quarters where GDPC1 shows positive sequential growth over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in industrial production signals immediate supply-side contraction and weakening business activity, which produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of instances where industrial production drops more than 1.0% month-over-month.
